data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: Design principle focusing on the size and placement of interactive elements for ease of use.

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Simplifies user choices by reducing the number of options presented at one time.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Design approach that limits information chunks to enhance user memory retention.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Aesthetic designs are perceived as more usable, even if they are not necessarily more efficient.
data.technical_spec: Visually pleasing interfaces, engaging color schemes

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Expectations are based on other sites; the site should align with user expectations built elsewhere.
